# GLOSSARY.md â€” Methodology controlled vocabulary (Tensio)

Generated mirror of the methodology's own canon terms â€” the framework's
controlled vocabulary that every docstring and generated doc must use
consistently. Terminology drift is invisibility (R-glossary-sync-test).

Source: `spec/src/tensio/glossary.py:TERMS`. Domain-side business terms
(R-ids, axis slugs, stakeholders) live in `spec/content/graph.py` and are
listed in REQUIREMENTS.md / TENSIONS.md â€” not duplicated here.

---

## Sections (Â§-anchors)
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `Â§Requirement` | The requirement node â€” a claim the system shall satisfy. |
| `Â§Conflict` | The first-class connector NODE between requirements, carrying axis+context+steward. |
| `Â§Assumption` | A belief with its own lifecycle (HOLDS/UNCERTAIN/DEAD). |
| `Â§Axis` | A controlled-vocabulary entry naming a tension dimension. |
| `Â§Stakeholder` | Accountability facet: who owns a requirement or stewards a conflict. |
| `Â§Invariants` | Structural form of the tension graph (check_* functions). |
| `Â§Graph` | The TensionGraph container + traversal + loader. |
| `Â§Lifecycle` | The generic state-machine value-type â€” keystone for Requirement.status / Conflict.lifecycle / future Operator/Process lifecycles. |
| `Â§Operator` | The acting facet of a Stakeholder â€” owns a sub-domain, carries a context budget, and runs the closed loop. |
| `Â§ContextBudget` | The working-store ceiling of an Operator; the substrate is free. |
| `Â§Loop` | The closed loop: Stateâ†’Diagnosisâ†’Next-actionâ†’Actionâ†’regenerateâ†’State. |
| `Â§Glossary` | The methodology's controlled vocabulary (this module). |
| `Â§Proposal` | Structured operator-â†’-steward change proposals: the closed loop's ACT half. |

## Lifecycle states
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `DETECTED` | A conflict has been surfaced but no steward action yet. |
| `ACKNOWLEDGED` | Steward accepts the conflict is real and owns it. |
| `DECIDED` | Resolved with recorded rationale and/or a derived requirement. |
| `REVISIT_WHEN` | Parked with an explicit revisit condition (anti-relitigation). |

## Statuses
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `DRAFT` | Proposed, not yet accepted into the canon. |
| `SETTLED` | Accepted and currently held. |
| `OPEN` | Accepted-with-a-hole; carries a non-empty question. |
| `REJECTED` | Withdrawn; kept for history (anti-relitigation). |
| `HOLDS` | Assumption currently believed true. |
| `UNCERTAIN` | Assumption whose truth is unconfirmed. |
| `DEAD` | Assumption known false â€” dependents are surfaced as DRIFT_FALLOUT. |
| `PROSE` | Enforcement level: recorded only, unenforced (soft context-debt). |
| `STRUCTURAL` | Enforcement level: visible/addressable but no predicate fires. |
| `ENFORCED` | Enforcement level: a check_* invariant or test fires on violation. |

## Roles
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `steward` | Holds a conflict; by construction NOT the owner of any of its members. |
| `owner` | Stakeholder who defends a requirement or an assumption. |
| `operator` | An acting agent that owns a bounded sub-domain of the graph. |

## Concepts
| slug | definition |
|---|---|
| `latent connector` | A requirement pair that SHOULD have a Conflict node but doesn't â€” a heuristic suspicion. |
| `DRIFT_FALLOUT` | A DEAD assumption with live dependents â€” they must be revisited. |
| `crystallize` | Move knowledge from working context into the durable substrate (graph + invariants + docs). |
| `anchor` | A stable, short, typed identifier (R-/C-/A-/Â§) the operator references rather than re-carries. |
| `substrate` | The durable, enforced/regenerable/addressable store â€” free of context cost. |
| `burn-down` | Promotion of DRAFT requirements to ENFORCED; the methodology's honesty governor. |
| `playbook` | A band-specific procedure the AI operator follows to convert a what_now action into a structured proposal for steward approval. |
| `apply_proposal` | The mechanical writer that lands a steward-approved proposal into spec/content/; the AI never edits the graph by hand. |
